title: The Reverie Opens Under Hidden Shadows
description: On pages 10–13, this part focuses on Unknown person #8, Saint Charlos, Shirahoshi, Unknown person #9, Unknown person #10, Reverie Delegates as The Reverie gathers world leaders while hidden powers at Mariejois begin to move.
scene: World leaders gather at Mariejois while the Revolutionary Army and the secret ruler behind the Empty Throne move in the shadows.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And why should I care?! Let me go!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: D'ya have no common sense?! If you anger him, all of dress- Rosa could be destroyed!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: I caught a big mer- maid!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: She's gonna be my pet! Mufungh!:)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Why are you all just standing around?! How dare he do that to a friend of the boss!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Damn and blast it! You fool!

dialogue.speaker: Shirahoshi
dialogue.text: We'll save you now, Mermaid Princess!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Who the hell are you lot?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Know that US stopping you just now saved several countries from utter destruction.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even kids know not to mess with celestial dragons.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The celestial dragons are the gods that created our world.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In effect, this man is one of the kings of this earth.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The wants of a God are to be yielded to him. It is inexorable.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: A God need not to make sense.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Give Shirahoo- Shi Back! She's my friend!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Thank you! I shall not forget your kindness.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But it is with a heavy heart... that I must give up.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I will beat these fools to a pulp!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And then we will return to the sea floor!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If this is what the world above is like, I cannot bring my people here!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: No! This isn't what it is like!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: This isn't what humans are like!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Only a little longer until we're all under the sun!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: You dare fight back? Kill them, CPO!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: We'll all take them on at once!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Maybe then, just maybe, our children's future

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Father! No! You mustn't hate another!

summary: This section advances the Reverie storyline with 56 dialogue beats. The Reverie looks ceremonial on the surface, but Imu, the Five Elders, and the Revolutionaries turn it into a major political pivot.
